rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture and insert new content only within a valid owner scope.
Keep type-level contract content with the component type; keep implementation-level bindings with the implementation scope.
Do not move or alter existing subcomponents, connections, features, properties, or ownership structure during insertion.
generic_rules: Respect owner visibility: type scopes should reference only symbols valid in that scope, not implementation-only paths.
Choose the minimal edit that adds the generated annex content while leaving surrounding declarations unchanged.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is wrapped in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Implementation-scoped AGREE content belongs after relevant structural declarations in the implementation body when that scope is required.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-only references into a type-owned annex.
Do not rewrite architecture elements or introduce unrelated structural changes just to host the annex.
irrelevant_topics: Requirement threshold selection or clause derivation details
AADL property names as AGREE expression variables unless explicitly exposed in model scope
